messages.text: We did a bulk product instead of an emulsion. It begins curing even at room temperature almost instantly. We will know in about an hour where this version stands over the emulsion

Here is a video for the bulk product. i believe we are at a point where we have a slight proof of concept where we have something that meets viscosity target and cure times but material properties we are not there

this product is going to need to go through more development and is not going to be turn-key therefor like all the other products this should go through a Stage Gate process before we continue spending time on this product
The tensile strength likely needs to be increased more and the elongation will definitely need to be addressed

<@U06F97BPQJU> I'm going to agree with Baker on this one. I think we've demonstrated that we can do something very similar mechanistically (low viscosity fluid, room temperature cure, < 3 hours cure time, etc.), but it appears that our primary concerns about the material having lower elongation, and perhaps a lower strength are accurate.

On a technical level, I definitely think we can get there, and my gut says that if they are using styrene monomer and methyl methacrylate monomer (they definitely are), in the field then from an environmental/worker safety standpoint this could be a huge benefit, but it's going to take more resources to hit the mechanical strength targets.

Baker, thanks for kicking this one…here is what I suggest, we already know a bunch of gate 2 and 3 answers, like the economics, base volumes and opportunities for this type of coating…we need to step back a layout a plan for what how we achieve the physical properties needed by the spec. Let’s be reasonable and thoughtful about time needed. I will communicate back to Nil at Polyset. Remember, they will probably make this product…

<@U06F97BPQJU> Baker has agreed to own the stage gate process on this one. We've started a template for this one. He's going to be harassing you to fill in the commercial/market/financial stuff, because it may be known, but I definitely don't know it.

<@U012ETLV6NQ> here's the link: <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qQMXKGI2f8lbHGXqV0Yf4ZVtLmER4aT9kttxUoVqMyg/edit>

Per michaels link to the PCO stagegate process.

<@U012212P2PQ> <@U06F97BPQJU>

please help me fill some of this in, and i will be realistic on a technology layout plant to hit some of the specs we are lacking in
i have linked the document at the top
Filled some of it out…plenty for what we’re doing.

Sounds good. Baker is planning on filling in the technical parts, and providing a determination on what level of resource that will be needed to complete. <@U012ETLV6NQ> When are you going to have time to turn this around?
i am going to try and finish this up by end of the week.

We can't make this too hard...the gate process can move quickly with a gate meeting. I will chair the meetings and we with this product, 2&3 are known...so really just down to can we make it and whats the plan and guestinate timeline. 
Always remember we can call a gate meeting to speed up or slow down a project.
I would say 1/2 FTE for 3 months likely gets this to a product that has a high chance of commercial success
Ok
Once you know what your plan is, I'll let Nil at Polyset know...might do a conference call

sounds good. one thing i will likely ask everybody for is just some general ideas on polymers/additives or approaches we can use to tune properties. that is what i will work on after i fill out the stage gate process. I dont want to deviate from what has been decided as "The Process"

<https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1mSPD7qLOrAUH40UALlM6_pyu2O_IGObSXusSZCxyLtk/edit#slide=id.p>

i have put together a general procedure as well as a quick next steps. everybody feel free to comment on what they think might be the correct direction. I have listed 3 potential directions
<@U04V1L47P7Y> has joined the channel
<@U012ETLV6NQ> Would you summarize the steps from the slides into slack and put anticipated dates for completing the formulation test?
Yeah I can do that tomorrow
